verses.text: The captain of the guard frees Jeremiah from the chains, owning that the LORD brought this evil for their sin, and bids him go where he will. Even a pagan acknowledges God. The prophet's word is vindicated before his captors.

The captain of the guard frees Jeremiah from the chains, owning that the LORD brought this evil for their sin, and bids him go where he will. Even a pagan acknowledges God. The prophet's word is vindicated before his captors.

The captain of the guard frees Jeremiah from the chains, owning that the LORD brought this evil for their sin, and bids him go where he will. Even a pagan acknowledges God. The prophet's word is vindicated before his captors.
Jeremiah chooses to stay with Gedaliah at Mizpah among the remnant in the land. He casts his lot with his people. The faithful pastor remains with the flock that is left.
Jeremiah chooses to stay with Gedaliah at Mizpah among the remnant in the land. He casts his lot with his people. The faithful pastor remains with the flock that is left.

The scattered captains gather to Gedaliah, who urges them, 'Fear not to serve the Chaldeans; dwell in the land,' and it shall be well. Wise counsel. Submission to God's appointed discipline brings peace.

The scattered captains gather to Gedaliah, who urges them, 'Fear not to serve the Chaldeans; dwell in the land,' and it shall be well. Wise counsel. Submission to God's appointed discipline brings peace.
The Jews who had fled to neighbouring lands return, and gather wine and summer fruits 'very much.' A measure of plenty returns. God grants the humbled remnant a season of provision.
The Jews who had fled to neighbouring lands return, and gather wine and summer fruits 'very much.' A measure of plenty returns. God grants the humbled remnant a season of provision.
Johanan warns Gedaliah that Ishmael is sent to kill him, but Gedaliah will not believe it. He trusts too easily. Honest men sometimes ignore real danger to their own hurt.
